summary: Completed testing of Next.js 16 LMS CozyTech application. Fixed critical Edge runtime error in middleware. All 17 API tests passed. Frontend pages (landing, login, register, dashboard, admin) render correctly.

minor.issue: Requires authentication - returns 401 for unauthenticated users (expected behavior)
Admin-only endpoint - returns 401 for non-admin users (expected behavior)

issues: User name shows 'Member Name' placeholder instead of actual user name from session
Stats show 0 values with 'Data akan dimuat dari API' - needs API integration

action_items: Dashboard page should display actual user name from session instead of 'Member Name' placeholder
Admin dashboard stats should fetch real data from API endpoints
Admin kelas page should fetch and display kelas list from /api/kelas
critical_code_review_comments: FIXED: middleware.ts was using auth() which imports bcrypt (Node.js module) causing Edge runtime error. Created auth.config.ts for Edge-compatible middleware
Dashboard components (navigasi.tsx, header.tsx) use lowercase function names - should be PascalCase for React components
Dashboard page.tsx has hardcoded user data - should use session data
Admin pages have placeholder data - need to implement API data fetching
seed_data_creation: POST /api/seed creates admin user, test user, and 3 sample kelas
context_for_next_testing_agent: This is a Next.js 16.1.1 app (not React+FastAPI). Auth uses NextAuth v5 beta.31. Middleware was fixed to use Edge-compatible auth config. All API endpoints work. Dashboard and admin pages render but have placeholder data that needs API integration.

rca_of_the_issue.issue: Edge runtime error: 'The edge runtime does not support Node.js stream module'
rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: middleware.ts was importing auth() from auth.ts which imports bcrypt (a Node.js module). Edge runtime doesn't support Node.js modules.
rca_of_the_issue.fix_applied: Created auth.config.ts with Edge-compatible NextAuth config using the 'authorized' callback pattern. Updated middleware.ts to use NextAuth's built-in middleware wrapper instead of manually calling auth().
rca_of_the_issue.verification: Login page now loads correctly, authentication flow works, protected routes redirect properly.
